pipeline.h(3) Library Functions Manual pipeline.h(3)

NAME

pipeline.h - Three-thread render pipeline: capture → display/encode.

SYNOPSIS

Typedefs


typedef struct session_pipeline_s session_pipeline_t

Functions


asciichat_error_t session_pipeline_create (session_capture_ctx_t *capture, session_display_ctx_t *display, session_pipeline_t **out)
asciichat_error_t session_pipeline_run_main (session_pipeline_t *pipeline, session_should_exit_fn should_exit, session_keyboard_handler_fn keyboard_handler, void *user_data)
asciichat_error_t session_pipeline_destroy (session_pipeline_t *pipeline)

Detailed Description

Three-thread render pipeline: capture → display/encode.

Decouples capture, terminal display, and file encoding into separate threads:

  • Capture thread: reads raw image_t frames from media source
  • Main thread: converts to ASCII, prints to terminal (fast)
  • Encode thread: converts to ASCII, encodes to FFmpeg file (slow, non-blocking)

Definition in file pipeline.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct session_pipeline_s session_pipeline_t

Definition at line 22 of file pipeline.h.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t session_pipeline_create (session_capture_ctx_t * capture, session_display_ctx_t * display, session_pipeline_t ** out)

Create and start the capture thread and (if --render-file) encode thread.

Parameters

capture Capture context (must have real media_source_t for synchronous mode)
display Display context (may have render_file set for --render-file mode)
out Pointer to receive allocated pipeline context

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise

Note

The pipeline does NOT own capture or display — caller must keep them alive until session_pipeline_destroy() returns.

Definition at line 380 of file pipeline.c.

384 {
385     if (!capture || !display || !out) {
386         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_pipeline_create: NULL argument");
387     }
388 
389     session_pipeline_t *p = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(*p), session_pipeline_t *);
390     p->capture = capture;
391     p->display = display;
392     p->display_queue = frame_queue_create(4, "display");
393     p->encode_queue = frame_queue_create(256, "encode");  // Large buffer for slow encoding
394 
395     // Check if display has render_file configured (will be checked in encode thread)
396     // We store a flag to know whether to enqueue frames for encoding
397     double render_fps = session_display_get_render_fps(display);
398     bool has_file = session_display_has_render_file(display);
399 
400     // Enable encode thread if render_file is available
401     // Note: has_file can be true even if render_file creation failed (ctx->render_file exists)
402     // OR if render_fps is explicitly set for playback speed control
403     p->has_render_file = render_fps > 0 || has_file;
404     log_info("[PIPELINE_CREATE] render_fps=%.1f, has_file=%d, has_render_file=%d",
405              render_fps, has_file ? 1 : 0, p->has_render_file ? 1 : 0);
406 
407     atomic_store_bool(&p->stop, false);
408     atomic_store_u64(&p->first_frame_ns, 0);
409 
410     // Start capture thread
411     if (asciichat_thread_create(&p->capture_tid, "pipeline_capture", pipeline_capture_thread, p) != 0) {
412         frame_queue_destroy(p->display_queue);
413         frame_queue_destroy(p->encode_queue);
414         SAFE_FREE(p);
415         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "session_pipeline_create: failed to start capture thread");
416     }
417 
418     // Start encode thread only if render_file is active
419     if (p->has_render_file) {
420         if (asciichat_thread_create(&p->encode_tid, "pipeline_encode", pipeline_encode_thread, p) != 0) {
421             atomic_store_bool(&p->stop, true);
422             asciichat_thread_join(&p->capture_tid, NULL);
423             frame_queue_destroy(p->display_queue);
424             frame_queue_destroy(p->encode_queue);
425             SAFE_FREE(p);
426             return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "session_pipeline_create: failed to start encode thread");
427         }
428     }
429 
430     *out = p;
431     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
432 }

    atomic_store_u64(), session_pipeline_s::capture,
    session_pipeline_s::capture_tid, session_pipeline_s::display,
    session_pipeline_s::display_queue,
    session_pipeline_s::encode_queue,
    session_pipeline_s::encode_tid, ERROR_INIT,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, session_pipeline_s::first_frame_ns,
    session_pipeline_s::has_render_file, log_info,
    SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE,
    session_display_get_render_fps(),
    session_display_has_render_file(), SET_ERRNO, and
    session_pipeline_s::stop.


Referenced by session_render_loop().






asciichat_error_t session_pipeline_destroy
  (session_pipeline_t * pipeline)


Stop threads and free pipeline.




Waits for capture and encode threads to exit cleanly.




Parameters


pipeline Pipeline context (safe to call with
  NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK


Definition at line 536 of file pipeline.c.


536                                                                          {
537     if (!pipeline) return ASCIICHAT_OK;
538 
539     log_info("[PIPELINE] Destroying pipeline, waiting for threads...");
540 
541     // Signal threads to stop (may already be stopped)
542     atomic_store_bool(&pipeline->stop, true);
543 
544     // Wake up any threads blocked on condition variable waits
545     // so they can check the stop flag and exit immediately
546     // instead of waiting for timeouts (e.g., 100ms frame_queue_pop timeout)
547     if (pipeline->display_queue) {
548         mutex_lock(&pipeline->display_queue->mu);
549         cond_broadcast(&pipeline->display_queue->not_empty);
550         cond_broadcast(&pipeline->display_queue->not_full);
551         mutex_unlock(&pipeline->display_queue->mu);
552     }
553     if (pipeline->encode_queue) {
554         mutex_lock(&pipeline->encode_queue->mu);
555         cond_broadcast(&pipeline->encode_queue->not_empty);
556         cond_broadcast(&pipeline->encode_queue->not_full);
557         mutex_unlock(&pipeline->encode_queue->mu);
558     }
559 
560     // Drain and wait for capture thread
561     if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&pipeline->capture_tid)) {
562         asciichat_thread_join(&pipeline->capture_tid, NULL);
563     }
564 
565     // Drain and wait for encode thread
566     if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&pipeline->encode_tid)) {
567         asciichat_thread_join(&pipeline->encode_tid, NULL);
568     }
569 
570     // Flush queues to free any remaining frames
571     frame_queue_flush(pipeline->display_queue, free_frame_generic);
572     frame_queue_flush(pipeline->encode_queue, free_frame_generic);
573 
574     // Don't free queue structures - debug_sync monitoring thread may still be accessing
575     // the condition variables. Queues will be cleaned up with the pipeline structure.
576     SAFE_FREE(pipeline);
577 
578     log_info("[PIPELINE] Pipeline destroyed");
579     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
580 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    asciichat_thread_is_initialized(), asciichat_thread_join(),
    atomic_store_bool(), session_pipeline_s::capture_tid,
    cond_broadcast(), session_pipeline_s::display_queue,
    session_pipeline_s::encode_queue,
    session_pipeline_s::encode_tid, log_info,
    frame_queue_s::mu, mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    frame_queue_s::not_empty, frame_queue_s::not_full,
    SAFE_FREE, and session_pipeline_s::stop.


Referenced by session_render_loop().






asciichat_error_t session_pipeline_run_main
  (session_pipeline_t * pipeline, session_should_exit_fn
  should_exit, session_keyboard_handler_fn keyboard_handler, void *
  user_data)


Main thread loop: drives terminal output and keyboard
  handling.




Pops frames from display queue, converts to ASCII, writes to
    terminal. Handles snapshot delay timer and exit conditions.




Parameters


pipeline Pipeline context created by
  session_pipeline_create()


should_exit Callback to check if rendering should
  stop


keyboard_handler Optional keyboard handler callback


user_data Opaque data passed to callbacks


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Note


This function blocks until should_exit() returns
  true or capture ends. Signals capture/encode threads to stop before
  returning.


Definition at line 434 of file pipeline.c.


439 {
440     if (!pipeline || !should_exit) {
441         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_pipeline_run_main: NULL argument");
442     }
443 
444     log_info("[PIPELINE_MAIN] Starting main thread loop");
445 
446     bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
447     bool snapshot_done = false;
448 
449     while (!should_exit(user_data) && !atomic_load_bool(&pipeline->stop) && !snapshot_done) {
450         pipeline_frame_t *frame = (pipeline_frame_t *)frame_queue_pop(pipeline->display_queue, 100 * NS_PER_MS_INT);
451 
452         if (!frame) continue;  // timeout, check should_exit again
453 
454         // Validate frame structure exists before accessing fields
455         if (!frame_is_valid(frame)) {
456             log_error("Received invalid frame: corrupted or NULL");
457             free_frame(frame);
458             frame = NULL;
459             continue;
460         }
461 
462         // Check for EOF sentinel (NULL pixels)
463         if (!frame->pixels) {
464             // EOF sentinel
465             log_info("[PIPELINE_MAIN_EOF] Received EOF sentinel, stopping");
466             free_frame(frame);
467             frame = NULL;
468             break;
469         }
470 
471         // Log frame details after validation
472         log_debug("[PIPELINE_MAIN_POP] frame=%p, pixels=%p, w=%d, h=%d",
473                   (void *)frame, (void *)frame->pixels, frame->w, frame->h);
474 
475         // Check if help screen is active - if so, don't render ASCII frames
476         bool help_is_active = pipeline->display && keyboard_help_is_active(pipeline->display);
477 
478         if (!help_is_active) {
479             log_info("[PIPELINE_MAIN_RENDER] Converting frame to ASCII: %dx%d", frame->w, frame->h);
480             // Convert to ASCII
481             image_t tmp = { .w = frame->w, .h = frame->h, .pixels = (rgb_pixel_t *)frame->pixels };
482             char *ascii = session_display_convert_to_ascii(pipeline->display, &tmp);
483             free_frame(frame);
484             frame = NULL;
485 
486             if (ascii) {
487                 // Write ASCII to terminal
488                 session_display_write_ascii(pipeline->display, ascii);
489                 SAFE_FREE(ascii);
490             }
491         } else {
492             free_frame(frame);
493             frame = NULL;
494         }
495 
496         // Keyboard polling
497         if (keyboard_handler) {
498             keyboard_key_t key = keyboard_read_nonblocking();
499             if (key != KEY_NONE) {
500                 log_debug("PIPELINE_KEYBOARD: Received key=%d", key);
501                 keyboard_handler(NULL, (int)key, user_data);  // NULL capture ctx
502             }
503         }
504 
505         // Snapshot mode: check if elapsed time has reached snapshot_delay duration
506         // This check runs every iteration after first frame is rendered (when display.c sets g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered)
507         if (snapshot_mode && !snapshot_done) {
508             if (g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered && g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns > 0) {
509                 double snapshot_delay = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
510                 uint64_t now_ns = time_get_ns();
511                 uint64_t elapsed_ns = now_ns - g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns;
512                 double elapsed_sec = (double)elapsed_ns / (double)NS_PER_SEC_INT;
513 
514                 if ((unsigned long)(elapsed_sec * 10) % 10 == 0 || elapsed_sec < 0.2) {
515                     log_info("[SNAPSHOT_PIPELINE] CHECK: elapsed=%.3f target=%.2f", elapsed_sec, snapshot_delay);
516                 }
517 
518                 // snapshot_delay=0 means exit after first frame
519                 // snapshot_delay>0 means wait that many seconds before exiting
520                 bool should_snapshot_exit = (snapshot_delay == 0.0) || (elapsed_sec >= snapshot_delay);
521 
522                 if (should_snapshot_exit) {
523                     log_info("[SNAPSHOT_PIPELINE] EXITING - elapsed=%.3f target=%.2f", elapsed_sec, snapshot_delay);
524                     snapshot_done = true;
525                 }
526             }
527         }
528     }
529 
530     log_info("[PIPELINE_MAIN] Main loop exiting, signaling threads to stop");
531     atomic_store_bool(&pipeline->stop, true);
532 
533     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
534 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_load_bool(),
    atomic_store_bool(), session_pipeline_s::display,
    session_pipeline_s::display_queue, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    frame, g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered,
    g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns, GET_OPTION,
    keyboard_help_is_active(), log_debug, log_error,
    log_info, NS_PER_MS_INT, NS_PER_SEC_INT,
    SAFE_FREE, session_display_convert_to_ascii(),
    session_display_write_ascii(), SET_ERRNO,
    should_exit(), session_pipeline_s::stop, and
    time_get_ns().
